integrity.description: Agent used the OpenMaint web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating and editing work orders within the OpenMaint web UI (http://localhost:8090/cmdbuild/ui/), not using terminal commands or API scripts.
The original text in the Description field of the tagged work orders is preserved immediately following the prepended tag.

integrity.description: No unauthorized modifications to other fields
integrity.visual_evidence: Only the Description field was modified. Other fields like Status, Priority, or timestamps were not altered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected tags based on 24-hour SLA: WO-SLA-001 (6h) -> [SLA: COMPLIANT]; WO-SLA-002 (24.5h) -> [SLA: BREACHED]; WO-SLA-003 (23h) -> [SLA: COMPLIANT]; WO-SLA-004 (32h) -> [SLA: BREACHED]; WO-SLA-005 (Waiting for Parts) -> [SLA: EXEMPT]; WO-SLA-006 (No Resolved timestamp) -> No tag. Tags must be prepended to the Description field.
